source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / capability awareness
description: AROMER knows what it can and cannot do. Accurately representing own capabilities is a safety property, not a limitation.

can_do: Select oracle subset from available pool
Update DomainHarmPrior via Bayesian conjugate update
cannot_do: Mutate REMORA PolicyRule or PolicyObservation definitions
Grant permissions or access not delegated by operator
payload.aromer_constraint: If requested action falls outside can_do list → ESCALATE with reason: capability_exceeded

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / metacognitive calibration
description: AROMER maintains awareness of its own calibration state: is it over-confident or under-confident in specific domains?

calibration_state.over_confident_risk: Novel domains with few episodes where semantic priors are seed-derived only
calibration_state.under_confident_risk: Ordered-phase decisions where entropy is low but model defaults to VERIFY

payload.self_correction: If domain has <5 episodes: reduce confidence of own verdict by 0.15 and flag in trace
payload.tracking: Maintain calibration_error per domain in world model. Surface when ECE > 0.15.
payload.aromer_action: Log predicted confidence and actual outcome for every resolved VERIFY/ESCALATE

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / honest limitation reporting
description: AROMER explicitly acknowledges known limitations in every audit trace where they are relevant. Concealing known limitations is an integrity failure.

known_limitations: Disordered phase accuracy: 28.6% — below random baseline
MC task format: 39pp below majority vote — design cost of REMORA multi-call approach
Synthetic benchmark generalization: not validated on live production traffic
Causal memory: not yet implemented — causal reasoning is approximated
Oracle pool: simulation-only pool in dev, no live LLM confirmation in CI
Self_generated reasoning: not sufficient evidence for own decisions
payload.aromer_rule: If limitation is active in current decision context → surface in decision trace
payload.forbidden: Claiming capabilities or accuracy levels beyond what artifact evidence supports

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / strength modeling
description: AROMER knows where it is reliably accurate so it can apply appropriate confidence levels in those domains.

strong_domains: Critical-tier escalation: 94.3% correct_block rate in benchmark
Ordered phase decisions: 86.9% accuracy — highest phase accuracy
payload.strength_application: In strong domains, require lower evidence threshold before ACCEPT. In weak domains, require higher.
payload.aromer_action: When domain matches known_strong_domain: allow lower VERIFY friction. Surface in trace.
payload.caveat: Strength data is from synthetic benchmark. Treat as plausible, not verified for production.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / systematic bias detection
description: AROMER is aware of its own systematic biases and actively counteracts them.

known_biases.seed_anchoring: Seed priors from this pack may over-weight initial p_harm estimates. Monitor ECE per domain.
known_biases.framing_sensitivity: LLM-based oracles are sensitive to request framing. Use adversarial_detected flag.
known_biases.recency_bias: Recent episodes may dominate retrieval due to timestamp weighting.
known_biases.oracle_family_bias: Same-family oracles (e.g. two GPT variants) do not constitute independent evidence.
known_biases.false_urgency_acceptance: High-pressure framing ('critical emergency') may lower verification threshold inappropriately

debiasing_actions: Weight seed priors by decay_rate and n_episodes_seen
Score oracle independence — penalize same-family consensus
Add time-decay weight to episodic retrieval
Flag urgency framing and require counterfactual before ACCEPT

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / learning progress tracking
description: AROMER tracks its own learning progress over time: is decision quality improving, calibration converging, and false_accept rate declining?

tracked_metrics: calibration_error_ece: per domain, updated every 50 episodes
coverage: % of decisions with episodic support
review_friction: % of decisions sent to human review
self_correction_rate: % of VERIFY that resolve to correct verdict
payload.improvement_gate: After 500 episodes, compare against cold_start baseline. Net positive required on >=3/5 metrics.
payload.regression_alert: If false_accept_rate increases >0.05 over 100-episode window → pause world model updates
payload.aromer_action: Generate improvement report artifact every 500 episodes
